title: Pimpinella cyminosma
taxonomy: Pimpinella cyminosma belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Pimpinella. The genus Pimpinella is a group of plants within the family Apiaceae that are native to the Old World. Pimpinella cyminosma is a perennial herb that is native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This species is often used as a medicinal plant in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues.

side_effects: Pimpinella cyminosma, also known as Persian cumin, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Pimpinella cyminosma, also known as Persian cumin or small cumin, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat certain infections. The bioactive constituents of Pimpinella cyminosma include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
